name: Virata
description: Virata was the king of the Matsya Kingdom, a prominent figure in the epic Mahabharata. He is renowned for his righteousness, hospitality, and bravery. During the Pandavas' thirteenth year of exile, which had to be spent incognito, they sought refuge in Virata's kingdom. Yudhishthira, Bhima, Arjuna, Nakula, and Sahadeva, along with Draupadi, disguised themselves and served in Virata's court under various pseudonyms. Virata unknowingly provided shelter and employment to the Pandavas, treating them with respect and generosity. He appointed Yudhishthira as his advisor, Bhima as his cook and wrestler, Arjuna as a dance instructor, Nakula as a horse caretaker, Sahadeva as a cattle caretaker, and Draupadi as a maid to his queen, Sudeshna. The Pandavas maintained their anonymity throughout this period, successfully completing their exile. Later, when the Pandavas revealed their true identities, Virata was overwhelmed with joy and offered his daughter, Uttara, in marriage to Arjuna's son, Abhimanyu, solidifying a strong alliance. Virata fought valiantly on the Pandava side in the Kurukshetra War and was ultimately slain by Drona. His role in providing sanctuary to the Pandavas during their crucial period of exile and his unwavering support for dharma mark him as a significant and honorable character in the Mahabharata, embodying the virtues of a just and compassionate ruler.
subtitle: Righteous King, Protector of the Pandavas
achievements: Provided refuge to the Pandavas during their exile
Maintained a just and prosperous kingdom
Offered his daughter in marriage to Abhimanyu
Fought bravely in the Kurukshetra War
Embodied the virtues of a righteous king

enemies.name: Drona (slain by him in the Kurukshetra war)

keyMyths.title: Sheltering the Pandavas
keyMyths.description: During their exile, the Pandavas sought refuge in Virata's kingdom, disguised as commoners. Virata unknowingly provided them with shelter and employment, showcasing his hospitality and righteousness.

keyMyths.title: Uttara's Marriage
keyMyths.description: After the Pandavas revealed their identities, Virata offered his daughter, Uttara, in marriage to Abhimanyu, Arjuna's son. This alliance strengthened the Pandava's position and ensured Virata's continued support.

keyMyths.title: Death in Kurukshetra
keyMyths.description: Virata fought valiantly on the Pandava side in the Kurukshetra War, displaying his bravery and loyalty. He was ultimately killed by Drona, sacrificing himself for the cause of dharma.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Virata and the Pandavas' exile.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Virata and the Pandavas' exile.
